text: The surgeon disassembles the ileostomy, dividing it into two sections, with one prepared for rhinostomosis. The mesentery near the intestine is sectioned, and the terminal ileum and cecum are completely released using mechanical energy.

The surgeon releases the cecum from adhesions using bipolar cautery, cold scissors, and blunt dissection. The adhesions between the vaginal dome and the cecum are completely separated, and the right ureter is identified. Urethrolysis is performed, fully separating the cecum from the ureter.

The surgeon carefully separates the blind from the peritoneal leaves while ensuring the ureter remains uninvolved. The parietal peritoneum is released, followed by the rectum, allowing for the complete release of the blind intended for extirpation. The intact vascularization of the remaining intestine is visible due to the precise dissection technique.

The surgeon identifies the cleavage plane and places a mechanical suture to completely section the ascending colon. The change in coloration between the tissue being resected and the tissue remaining for regrastomosis is visible. The resected segment is then extracted through the ileostomy site.

The surgeon makes an incision at the ascending colon and another at the terminal ileum, using the previous heliostomy mark as a guide. Linear grooves are placed in each opening, facilitating the reanastomosis of the ileum with the ascending colon. The procedure concludes with an intracorporeal suture to close the remaining gap in a return and return plane.
